rules.explanation: Certain nouns like scenery, poetry, furniture, advice, information, hair, and business are uncountable. They do not have a plural form and do not take 'a' or 'an' directly.
examples: The scenery of Switzerland is beautiful.
rules.wrong_example: The sceneries of Kashmir are enchanting.
rules.correct_example: The scenery of Kashmir is enchanting.
rules.exam_tip: Never use 'sceneries', 'furnitures', or 'informations' in your sentences.
rules.common_error: Adding 's' to uncountable nouns to make them plural.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: Frequently seen in SSC CGL and Bank Clerk spotting error sections.
rules.practice_question: Identify the error: The furnitures (A) / in this shop (B) / are very (C) / expensive (D).

rules.topic: Plural in Form, Singular in Meaning
rules.rule_title: Nouns ending in 's' but treated as singular
rules.explanation: Subjects like Mathematics, Physics, Ethics, and diseases like Measles or Mumps look plural because they end in 's', but they represent a single unit/subject.
rules.wrong_example: Statistics are a branch of Mathematics.
rules.correct_example: Statistics is a branch of Mathematics.
rules.exam_tip: If these words are used to denote personal data or qualities (e.g., 'His mathematics'), they can take a plural verb.
rules.common_error: Using a plural verb just because the noun ends in 's'.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: Common in Railway and State PSC exams.
rules.practice_question: Politics ____ a dirty game. (A) is (B) are

rules.topic: Singular in Form, Plural in Meaning
rules.rule_title: Collective nouns that are always plural
rules.explanation: Nouns like Cattle, Gentry, Peasantry, Poultry, Clergy, and People look singular (no 's') but they always represent a plural group.
examples: Cattle are grazing in the field.
rules.wrong_example: The cattles are grazing.
rules.correct_example: The cattle are grazing.
rules.exam_tip: Do not add 's' to Cattle or Gentry. 'Peoples' means different races/nations, not the plural of person.
rules.common_error: Writing 'cattles' or using 'is' with these words.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: High frequency in SSC CHSL and Banking Prelims.
rules.practice_question: The poultry ____ been fed. (A) has (B) have

rules.explanation: In compound nouns, we make the principal (main) word plural, not the description word.
rules.wrong_example: I have two brother-in-laws.
rules.correct_example: I have two brothers-in-law.
rules.exam_tip: For 'Step-son', it is 'Step-sons' because 'son' is the main noun.
rules.common_error: Adding 's' at the very end of the compound word.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: UPSC CDS and NDA English papers.
rules.practice_question: Choose the correct plural: (A) Passer-bys (B) Passers-by

rules.explanation: If a noun is repeated after a preposition, both nouns must be in the singular form.
rules.wrong_example: Rows upon rows of marble look beautiful.
rules.correct_example: Row upon row of marble looks beautiful.
rules.exam_tip: The verb following this structure must also be singular.
rules.common_error: Using plural nouns on either side of the preposition.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: Common in Insurance and Banking exams.
rules.practice_question: He wandered from _____ . (A) doors to doors (B) door to door

rules.explanation: Collective nouns take a singular verb when they act as a single unit, but a plural verb when members are divided or acting individually.
rules.formula_or_pattern: United = Singular Verb; Divided = Plural Verb
examples: The jury has given its verdict.
The jury were divided in their opinions.
rules.wrong_example: The committee have submitted its report.
rules.correct_example: The committee has submitted its report.
rules.exam_tip: Look for the pronoun 'its' (singular) or 'their' (plural) to decide the verb.
rules.common_error: Always using a singular verb for collective nouns.
rules.practice_question: The audience ____ taken their seats. (A) has (B) have

rules.explanation: Nouns like pair, dozen, score, hundred, thousand, and million do not take 's' when preceded by a definite number.
examples: He gave me five hundred rupees.
rules.wrong_example: He spent five thousands rupees.
rules.correct_example: He spent five thousand rupees.
rules.exam_tip: If used indefinitely (e.g., 'Thousands of people'), then 's' is required.
rules.common_error: Saying 'two hundreds' instead of 'two hundred'.
rules.practice_question: He owns several ____ of land. (A) acre (B) acres

rules.explanation: Material nouns (gold, silver, iron, cotton) generally do not take an article (a, an, the) when used in a general sense.
rules.wrong_example: The gold is a precious metal.
rules.correct_example: Gold is a precious metal.
rules.exam_tip: Use 'The' only when specifying (e.g., 'The gold of this ring').
rules.common_error: Using 'the' before every material noun.
rules.practice_question: ____ iron is a useful metal. (A) A (B) The (C) No article

rules.explanation: We use ('s) for living things, but for non-living things, we use the 'of' phrase.
rules.formula_or_pattern: Living's + thing OR thing + of + Non-living
rules.wrong_example: The table's leg is broken.
rules.correct_example: The leg of the table is broken.
rules.exam_tip: Exceptions include personified objects (e.g., 'Nature's laws', 'Duty's call').
rules.common_error: Applying ('s) to chairs, tables, and fans.
rules.practice_question: Which is correct? (A) The building's roof (B) The roof of the building

rules.explanation: For plural nouns ending in 's', we only add an apostrophe (') without an extra 's'. For singular names ending in 's', both are acceptable but usually just (') is used.
rules.wrong_example: Boys's school
rules.correct_example: Boys' school
rules.exam_tip: Check if the noun is singular or plural before placing the apostrophe.
rules.common_error: Writing 'Girls's' for a girls' college.
rules.practice_question: This is a ______ college. (A) Ladies' (B) Lady's (C) Ladies's

rules.explanation: Some nouns exist only in plural forms and take plural verbs: Scissors, Trousers, Spectacles, Goggles, Pincers, Alms, Riches.
rules.wrong_example: Your spectacle is very nice.
rules.correct_example: Your spectacles are very nice.
rules.exam_tip: To make them singular, use 'A pair of...' (e.g., 'A pair of spectacles is...').
rules.common_error: Using 'is' with scissors or trousers.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: Very common in Railway Group D and SSC MTS.
rules.practice_question: His spectacles _____ been broken. (A) has (B) have

rules.explanation: Some nouns change their meaning entirely when an 's' is added.
rules.wrong_example: The tax was levied on good.
rules.correct_example: The tax was levied on goods.
rules.exam_tip: Context is key. 'Wood' is a material; 'Woods' is a forest.
rules.common_error: Confusing 'Force' (Strength) with 'Forces' (Army).
rules.previous_exam_pattern: UPSC Prelims and Bank PO Mains.
rules.practice_question: The prisoner was kept in ***. (A) iron (B) irons

rules.explanation: A hyphenated noun acting as an adjective never takes a plural form.
rules.wrong_example: He gave me two five-rupees notes.
rules.correct_example: He gave me two five-rupee notes.
rules.exam_tip: If the hyphen is missing, you might need a plural (e.g., 'Five rupees').
rules.common_error: Writing 'Five-years plan' instead of 'Five-year plan'.
rules.practice_question: Stay in a _____ hotel. (A) five-stars (B) five-star

rules.rule_title: Proper nouns used as Common nouns
rules.explanation: When a proper noun is used to represent a quality or for comparison, it acts as a common noun and takes 'the'.
examples: Kalidas is the Shakespeare of India.
Ahmedabad is the Manchester of India.
rules.wrong_example: He is a Newton of our class.
rules.correct_example: He is the Newton of our class.
rules.exam_tip: Regular proper nouns (names of people/places) usually don't take 'the'.
rules.common_error: Omitting 'the' during a comparison of two famous people.
rules.practice_question: Kashmir is ____ Switzerland of India. (A) a (B) an (C) the

rules.explanation: The phrase 'One of' is always followed by a plural noun, but the verb remains singular because the subject is 'One'.
rules.formula_or_pattern: One of + the + Plural Noun + Singular Verb
examples: One of my friends is a doctor.
One of the books is missing.
rules.wrong_example: One of my friend is a doctor.
rules.correct_example: One of my friends is a doctor.
rules.exam_tip: Always check for 's' after 'One of'. This is the most common error.
rules.common_error: Using a singular noun after 'One of'.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: Highest frequency in all competitive exams.
rules.practice_question: One of the _____ was absent. (A) student (B) students

rules.explanation: Abstract nouns (Honesty, Love, Hatred, Knowledge) are uncountable and take singular verbs.
rules.wrong_example: The honesties shown by him were great.
rules.correct_example: The honesty shown by him was great.
rules.exam_tip: You cannot count 'an honesty' or 'two knowledges'.
rules.common_error: Trying to pluralize feelings or qualities.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: Basic grammar checks in SSC and Defense.
rules.practice_question: Her _____ knows no bounds. (A) happiness (B) happinesses

rules.explanation: Nouns like teacher, student, child, clerk, and doctor are common gender. In formal English, use 'he/him/his' if the gender is unknown.
examples: Every student should do his duty.
A teacher should be honest to his work.
rules.wrong_example: Every student should do their duty. (Informal)
rules.correct_example: Every student should do his duty.
rules.exam_tip: In recent years, 'their' is becoming acceptable, but exams still prefer 'his'.
rules.common_error: Using 'their' with a singular common noun like 'student'.
rules.practice_question: The doctor came to see ____ patient. (A) his (B) their

rules.explanation: Avoid using two apostrophes in the same sentence back-to-back.
examples: The son of my friend's secretary.
The wife of my brother's friend.
rules.wrong_example: My friend's secretary's son has come.
rules.correct_example: The son of my friend's secretary has come.
rules.exam_tip: Rewrite the sentence using 'of' to make it clearer.
rules.common_error: Stringing multiple ('s) together.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: Bank PO and SSC CGL Tier 2.
rules.practice_question: Which is more appropriate? (A) My brother's friend's car (B) The car of my brother's friend

rules.explanation: Some words from Latin/Greek have unique plural endings. (um -> a, is -> es).
rules.formula_or_pattern: Criterion -> Criteria; Phenomenon -> Phenomena; Basis -> Bases
examples: Data is/are (Data is plural of Datum).
Crisis is singular, Crises is plural.
rules.wrong_example: All the criterions were met.
rules.correct_example: All the criteria were met.
rules.exam_tip: Remember: 'Criteria' and 'Phenomena' are PLURAL. Use plural verbs with them.
rules.common_error: Using 'criterias' or 'phenomenas'.
rules.practice_question: What is the plural of 'Analysis'? (A) Analysises (B) Analyses

rules.explanation: When a plural noun denotes a specific amount, distance, or weight as a single unit, use a singular verb.
examples: Ten miles is a long distance.
Five thousand rupees is a large sum.
rules.wrong_example: Fifty kilos are too heavy for me.
rules.correct_example: Fifty kilos is too heavy for me.
rules.exam_tip: If the units are being used individually (e.g., 'Five silver coins'), use a plural verb.
rules.common_error: Using 'are' with distances and money sums.
rules.practice_question: Two years ____ a long time to wait. (A) is (B) are

rules.explanation: Use 'Many' with countable nouns and 'Much' with uncountable nouns.
examples: How much milk do you need?
rules.wrong_example: I don't have many milk.
rules.correct_example: I don't have much milk.
rules.exam_tip: The same applies to 'Few' (Countable) and 'Little' (Uncountable).
rules.common_error: Using 'many' with money, water, or advice.
rules.practice_question: There isn't _____ sugar in the pot. (A) many (B) much

rules.explanation: Use 'The' before names of rivers, mountain ranges, oceans, and holy books.
examples: The Himalayas are in the north.
rules.wrong_example: Mount Everest is the part of Himalayas.
rules.correct_example: Mount Everest is part of the Himalayas.
rules.exam_tip: Do not use 'the' before individual peaks (e.g., Mount Everest). Use it for ranges.
rules.common_error: Omitting 'the' before 'Ganges' or 'Atlantic Ocean'.
rules.practice_question: *** Nile is the longest river. (A) A (B) The (C) No article

rules.explanation: Competitive exams often ask for the specific collective noun for groups.
rules.wrong_example: A group of keys.
rules.correct_example: A bunch of keys.
rules.exam_tip: Memorize common ones like 'A shoal of fish' or 'A constellation of stars'.
rules.common_error: Using 'group' for everything.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: One-word substitution sections in SSC and Defence.
rules.practice_question: A group of sailors is called a: (A) Team (B) Crew (C) Fleet

rules.explanation: To make nouns ending in '-ful' plural, add 's' at the end of the whole word.
rules.wrong_example: He took two spoonsful of sugar.
rules.correct_example: He took two spoonfuls of sugar.
rules.exam_tip: This is different from compound nouns like 'Brothers-in-law'.
rules.common_error: Writing 'handsful' or 'cupsful'.
rules.practice_question: The doctor advised two _____ of medicine. (A) mouthfuls (B) mouthsful

rules.explanation: Usually abstract nouns don't take an article, but when followed by 'of', they take 'the'.
examples: The honesty of Ram is praised by all.
The bravery of the soldiers won the war.
rules.wrong_example: Honesty of Ram is known to all.
rules.correct_example: The honesty of Ram is known to all.
rules.exam_tip: General: 'Honesty is best'. Specific: 'The honesty of...'
rules.common_error: Missing 'the' when defining a specific person's quality.
rules.practice_question: ____ courage shown by the boy was amazing. (A) A (B) The (C) No article

rules.explanation: In English, we only use 'cousin'. Using 'brother' or 'sister' with it is redundant and wrong.
rules.wrong_example: Ram is my cousin brother.
rules.correct_example: Ram is my cousin.
rules.exam_tip: The gender is understood from the name (Ram/Sita) or the pronoun (He/She).
rules.common_error: Saying 'Cousin sister' for a female cousin.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: Spoken English errors in SSC and KPSC.
rules.practice_question: Is she your _____? (A) cousin (B) cousin sister

rules.explanation: Words like News, Series, Innings, and Gallows look plural but are used as singular.
rules.wrong_example: These news are fake.
rules.correct_example: This news is fake.
rules.exam_tip: Even 'two innings' is correct, but 'one innings' is also correct (not 'one inning').
rules.common_error: Using 'are' with 'News'.
rules.practice_question: The first innings ____ just finished. (A) has (B) have

rules.explanation: When referring to fish as food/collection, use 'fish'. When referring to different species, use 'fishes'.
examples: There are many fish in the pond.
There are various fishes in the aquarium.
rules.wrong_example: I ate two fishes today.
rules.correct_example: I ate two fish today.
rules.exam_tip: In most exam cases, 'fish' is used as the plural form.
rules.common_error: Always using 'fishes' as the plural of fish.
rules.practice_question: The aquarium has many colorful ____. (A) fish (B) fishes

rules.explanation: 'Work' as an uncountable noun means labor. 'Works' means literary/artistic pieces or a factory.
examples: I have a lot of work to do.
I have read the works of Shakespeare.
rules.wrong_example: I have many works to finish today.
rules.correct_example: I have a lot of work to finish today.
rules.exam_tip: To make it countable, say 'A piece of work'.
rules.common_error: Saying 'many works' when referring to daily tasks.
rules.practice_question: She has much _____ to do. (A) work (B) works

rules.explanation: If two people own the same thing, add ('s) only to the second name. If they own different things, add ('s) to both.
rules.formula_or_pattern: A and B's [Joint] vs A's and B's [Separate]
examples: Ram's and Shyam's houses (Two separate houses).
rules.wrong_example: Ram's and Shyam's father is a doctor.
rules.correct_example: Ram and Shyam's father is a doctor. (Assuming they are brothers)
rules.exam_tip: If the noun following is singular, it's usually joint possession.
rules.common_error: Using ('s) on both names for a shared object.
rules.previous_exam_pattern: Bank PO Mains and SSC Tier 2.
rules.practice_question: This is ____ school. (A) Ram and Sita's (B) Ram's and Sita's
